metadata.category: Arrays | Prefix Sums | Binary Search | Geometry

content.problemStatement:
Chef found a matrix AAA with NNN rows (numbered 111 through NNN) and MMM columns (numbered 111 through MMM), where for each row rrr and column ccc, the cell in row rrr and column ccc (denoted by (r,c)(r,c)(r,c)) contains an integer Ar,cA_{r,c}Ar,c​.

This matrix has two interesting properties:


The integers in each row form a non-decreasing sequence, i.e. for each valid iii, Ai,1≤Ai,2≤…≤Ai,MA_{i,1} \leq A_{i,2} \leq \ldots \leq A_{i,M}Ai,1​≤Ai,2​≤…≤Ai,M​.
The integers in each column also form a non-decreasing sequence, i.e. for each valid jjj, A1,j≤A2,j≤…≤AN,jA_{1,j} \leq A_{2,j} \leq \ldots \leq A_{N,j}A1,j​≤A2,j​≤…≤AN,j​.
A KKK-worthy submatrix is a square submatrix of AAA, i.e. a submatrix with lll rows and lll columns, for any integer lll, such that the average of all the integers in this submatrix is ≥K\geq K≥K.

Chef wants you to find the number of KKK-worthy submatrices of AAA.
constraints: Rows are non‑decreasing, columns are non‑decreasing
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains three integers N, M and K. Then N lines follow, each containing M integers describing the matrix A. Rows and columns of A are non‑decreasing.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the number of square submatrices whose average is at least K.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The following are the seven 444-worthy submatrices:

[3445]
\begin{bmatrix}
3 & 4 \\
4 & 5 \\
\end{bmatrix}[34​45​]
with average 444; this matrix occurs only once
[4555]\begin{bmatrix}
4 & 5 \\
5 & 5 \\
\end{bmatrix}[45​55​]
with average 4.754.754.75; this matrix also occurs only once
[4]\begin{bmatrix}
4 \\
\end{bmatrix}[4​]
with average 444; we find this matrix twice in AAA
[5]\begin{bmatrix}
5 \\
\end{bmatrix}[5​]
with average 555; we find this matrix 333 times in AAA

solution.approach: For each cell (i, j) we treat it as the top‑left corner of a square. Define B[r][c] = A[r][c] - K. A square of size l has average ≥ K ⇔ sum of its B‑values ≥ 0. Because the matrix is non‑decreasing in rows and columns, when we enlarge a square its sum never decreases – we only add numbers that are ≥ the numbers already inside. Hence for a fixed top‑left corner the function f(l) = sum of the l×l square is monotone non‑decreasing. For each (i, j) we binary‑search the smallest l (1 ≤ l ≤ maxSize) such that f(l) ≥ 0. If such l exists, all larger sizes are also worthy, so we add (maxSize - l + 1) to the answer. Prefix sums of B allow O(1) retrieval of any square sum. Overall complexity per test case is O(N·M·log min(N,M)) with O(N·M) memory, easily fast for the given limits.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.buffer.read().split()
    it = iter(data)
    t = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(t):
        n = int(next(it)); m = int(next(it)); k = int(next(it))
        pref = [[0]*(m+1) for _ in range(n+1)]
        for i in range(1, n+1):
            row_sum = 0
            for j in range(1, m+1):
                a = int(next(it))
                b = a - k
                pref[i][j] = b + pref[i-1][j] + pref[i][j-1] - pref[i-1][j-1]
        ans = 0
        for i in range(1, n+1):
            for j in range(1, m+1):
                max_sz = min(n - i + 1, m - j + 1)
                lo, hi = 1, max_sz
                best = -1
                while lo <= hi:
                    mid = (lo + hi)//2
                    i2 = i + mid - 1
                    j2 = j + mid - 1
                    s = pref[i2][j2] - pref[i-1][j2] - pref[i2][j-1] + pref[i-1][j-1]
                    if s >= 0:
                        best = mid
                        hi = mid - 1
                    else:
                        lo = mid + 1
                if best != -1:
                    ans += max_sz - best + 1
        out_lines.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
